points: New York is the epicenter of BESS restrictions.

As of the June 2026 tracker refresh, New York had over 100 active local BESS moratoriums — more than 80% of every active BESS moratorium in the U.S. Westchester remains among the most-restricted counties. The coalition tracks 5 towns with an active moratorium (Bedford, Cortlandt, New Castle, the Village of Ossining, and Pound Ridge); Cortlandt's moratorium runs through Dec 31, 2026 per LL 7-2025, and Pound Ridge's through Sept 24, 2026. Mamaroneck Village concluded its moratorium drafting period and adopted a permanent restrictive code (LL 7-2026: Tier 1 ≤40/80 kWh; >80 kWh prohibited). Several towns have outright bans (Mount Pleasant, Mount Kisco, Somers).
Local control is decisive for standalone projects.

The State Office of Renewable Energy Siting (ORES) has no jurisdiction over standalone battery storage. A standalone BESS of any size must be permitted locally, so an active town moratorium or ban is binding with no state workaround. Only storage co-located with a solar/wind project ≥25 MW can use ORES to override local zoning.

Senate Bill S5506 (Kavanagh) and Assembly Bill A8378 (Levenberg) would expand ORES to standalone storage. Introduced Feb 2025, still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee as of June 2026, not passed either chamber. Several Westchester municipalities (e.g., Yorktown) passed resolutions opposing it.
Most moratoriums are temporary — but some harden into bans.

Moratoriums are usually a pause while a town writes zoning; a large NY cluster expires mid-to-late 2026. In Westchester, Mount Pleasant and Mount Kisco have moved from moratorium to effective bans, while Port Chester adopted permanent regulations instead. The next several months are the highest-leverage window to shape the permanent rules.

towns.name: Village of Briarcliff Manor
towns.scope: Tiered code — Tier 1 (≤600 kWh) by-right; Tier 2 (>600 kWh) allowed only by special-use permit under very restrictive siting
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester
snapshot: 75% Red, 25% Yellow — Low capacity (ConEd/NYSEG hosting-capacity map)

Village Code Chapter 178 (Art. II), §178-9: Tier 1 (≤600 kWh) permitted by-right in all districts (building permit, exempt from site plan); Tier 2 (>600 kWh) permitted only by Board-of-Trustees special-use permit + Planning Board site plan.

Tier 2 is not banned, but siting rules make it very hard: 5-acre minimum lot in residential zones, 50 ft property-line / 100 ft building setbacks, and 0.1-mile "avoidance" zones around schools, healthcare, senior-living, and municipal facilities.

A Village BESS Committee (Mayor, Village Manager, Engineer, DPW, Counsel, Fire & Police chiefs, Sustainability Advisory Council) issued Initial Recommendations refining Tier 1 and Tier 2 standards — the code is being actively worked.
summary: Briarcliff Manor regulates battery storage under Village Code Chapter 178: small Tier 1 systems (≤600 kWh) are permitted by-right in every district with a building permit, while large Tier 2 systems (>600 kWh) are allowed only through a Board-of-Trustees special-use permit plus Planning Board site-plan review. So the Village has a full tiered framework rather than an outright Tier 2 ban.

In practice the Tier 2 pathway is heavily constrained — a 5-acre minimum site in residential zones, large setbacks, and 0.1-mile avoidance buffers around schools, healthcare, senior housing, and municipal buildings — which, combined with low grid hosting capacity, makes utility-scale storage difficult to site. A standing Village BESS Committee has issued initial recommendations and continues to refine the rules, so there is an open, substantive process to engage.
committees: Board of Trustees — issues Tier 2 special-use permits and adopts any code amendments.
BESS Committee — Mayor, Village Manager, Village Engineer, DPW Superintendent, Village Counsel, Fire & Police chiefs, and the Sustainability Advisory Council; drafting/refining the BESS standards.

Kept as Restrictive for now because the Tier 2 siting rules (5-acre min, avoidance buffers) plus low grid capacity effectively block meaningful storage. Flag for the workgroup to decide.
Engagement angle: rather than "add a Tier 2 pathway," the ask here is to loosen the existing Tier 2 siting standards toward a workable, Croton-style framework.

A County Planning referral (BMR-25-005, 10/1/25) corresponds to Briarcliff’s 2025 BESS code activity — worth pulling for the exact adopted-vs-proposed Tier 2 language when reconciling the "prohibits Tier 2" vs "permitted by special-use permit" question above.

towns.scope: Restrictive — Type 1 (utility, >600 kWh) & Type 2 (commercial, 80–600 kWh) prohibited town-wide; only Type 3 (≤80 kWh residential) possibly allowed (LL 4-2024)
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester — Armonk, North White Plains, Banksville
snapshot: 25% Red, 75% Yellow — Moderate capacity (ConEd/NYSEG hosting-capacity map)

Local Law 4 of 2024 (adopted Aug 14, 2024) amended Town Code §355-4 to exclude BESS from "public utility" and define three types, then amended the permitted-use sections to prohibit them: §355-21 and §355-23 (IND-A) exclude Type 1 & Type 2; §355-22 (CB-A and GB business districts) excludes all BESS. Effect: no commercial or utility-scale BESS anywhere in Town.

Type 1 = utility (>600 kWh or grid-serving); Type 2 = commercial (80–600 kWh, on-site only, no sale of excess to the grid); Type 3 = residential (≤80 kWh). Type 1 & 2 are prohibited; Type 3 is neither permitted nor prohibited.

The Westchester County Planning Board (Referral NOC 24-008, July 1, 2024) found the law would "effectively ban BESS systems of Types 1 and 2," disagreed that a near-total ban is appropriate, and urged the Town to adopt the NYSERDA Model Law and clarify Type 3 status.
summary: North Castle adopted Local Law 4 of 2024 on August 14, 2024, which redefined "public utility" to exclude battery energy storage and amended the Town Code’s permitted-use sections to prohibit Type 1 (utility, >600 kWh) and Type 2 (commercial, 80–600 kWh) BESS in the residential and IND-A industrial zones, and to prohibit all BESS in the CB-A and GB business districts. Only Type 3 residential systems (≤80 kWh) are left unaddressed — neither permitted nor prohibited.

The Westchester County Planning Board reviewed the measure (Referral NOC 24-008) and called it an effective ban on Types 1 and 2, formally disagreeing with a near-total ban and recommending the NYSERDA Model Law instead. This confirms North Castle is a restrictive, near-ban jurisdiction — an engagement target to replace the prohibition with a real, well-sited tiered pathway, not a model to emulate.
flags: Primary sources confirmed (7/2/26): Town of North Castle Local Law 4 of 2024 (adopted 8/14/24) and Westchester County Planning Board Referral NOC 24-008 (7/1/24). The County itself characterized the law as an effective ban on Type 1 & Type 2 BESS.

CONFLICT to reconcile: the workgroup GIS sheet lists North Castle as "BESS allowed (3 Tiers)" (a misread of the §355-4 definitions, which the law uses to PROHIBIT Types 1 & 2). Recommend updating the sheet and removing any "model" designation.

Classification note: kept as "Restrictive" because Type 3 (≤80 kWh) residential systems are not expressly prohibited; the workgroup could reasonably treat this as a "Ban" given the County’s "effective ban" finding.

steps: Prioritize the towns mid-drafting — Cortlandt, New Castle, Pound Ridge, Bedford, Peekskill, Mamaroneck (Village), and Yonkers — where a permanent law is actively being written. Input now shapes the actual text.
Engage Greenburgh on its amendments. It already has a BESS law and is revising it via the Planning Board — an open, substantive process.
Get ahead in Rye Brook. With no moratorium yet, it is the best chance to help craft a balanced ordinance before a reactive ban.
Check Harrison and Yorktown's next moves. Both pauses lapsed; confirm whether permanent BESS codes are coming. Yorktown is also fighting state preemption (S5506/A8378).
Study Port Chester as a model. It chose permanent regulations over a ban — useful precedent and language for other towns.
Monitor the ban towns (Mount Pleasant, Mount Kisco). Bans can be revisited or challenged; watch for reconsideration or litigation.
Get on agendas in advance. Most boards require contacting the clerk/supervisor days ahead (Pound Ridge: 5 days, copy the Clerk). Public-comment periods are also available at each meeting.
Lead with the sustainability committees in each town for a “safe siting, not outright ban” message, and track the state bill S5506 / A8378.
sources: Westchester County Planning Board — 239-M Referrals Hub (map of municipal referrals, incl. BESS)
Carina Energy — NY BESS Moratoriums tracker (May 2026)
Town of Cortlandt — LL 2 of 2025 (moratorium extension)
Mamaroneck Village — Recently Adopted Local Laws 2025
Port Chester — Board of Trustees / Agenda Center
Mount Pleasant — press release: BESS ban
The Examiner — Mount Kisco BESS moratorium/ban

items: New Castle: verified via Westchester County Planning referral NWC-25-007 (10/28/25) that the proposed permanent law permits ONLY Tier 1 (≤80 kWh) and PROHIBITS Tier 2 & Tier 3 town-wide (the County objected to the ban). Corrected the scope line, which had described a permissive 3-tier framework; added the referral confirmation to snapshot/localLaw/flags. Status kept as Moratorium pending confirmation the Town Board has adopted the law — if adopted, reclassify to Restrictive/effective ban.

Scanned the County 239-M Referrals Hub for all BESS referrals and cross-checked: Mount Kisco (MTK-25-001) and Mount Pleasant (MTP-25-010) "BESS Prohibition" referrals corroborate their ban status; Cortlandt, Ossining Village, Mamaroneck Village, Larchmont, Yorktown, and Bedford referrals all consistent with current entries. Added confirming referral links to Mount Kisco, Mount Pleasant, New Castle, and Briarcliff (BMR-25-005).

North Castle: added primary-source detail from the adopted Local Law 4 of 2024 and Westchester County Planning Board Referral NOC 24-008 (both provided from email). LL 4-2024 (8/14/24) excludes BESS from "public utility" and prohibits Type 1 (>600 kWh) & Type 2 (80–600 kWh) in residential/IND-A zones and all BESS in the CB-A/GB business zones; the County called it an effective ban on Types 1 & 2 and recommended the NYSERDA Model Law. Corrected the Town Hall address (15 Bedford Road) and the earlier imprecise "systems >80 kW exempt" note.
Added the Westchester County Planning Department 239-M Referrals Hub (ArcGIS) as a project resource/source for tracking municipal BESS referrals.

North Castle RECLASSIFIED (major): moved from "BESS Allowed / model" to "Restrictive." A workgroup contact flagged that North Castle does not permit larger-scale BESS and received a 2024 ban referral; confirmed via the Examiner (8/27/24) that the Town adopted an 8/14/24 local law excluding BESS from the "public utility" definition, effectively barring utility-scale/standalone storage (household systems >80 kW exempt). The sheet’s "3 tiers allowed" came from §355-4 definitions, which only define — not permit — the tiers. Removed dr/model status. Conflict flagged for the workgroup to update the GIS sheet.

Deep-dived four restrictive codes and corrected contradictory entries: City of Rye (LL 2025-02 §98-124.1, Tier 1 <80 kWh only; new Mayor Josh Nathan since Jan 2026), Village of Larchmont (LL 2-2026, Tier 1 ≤600 kWh, Tier 2 prohibited), Town of North Salem (§250-13, on-site-only via public-utility exclusion), and Village of Rye Brook (LL 2-2025 §98-124.1, Tier 1 ≤80 kWh — corrected a prior "no ordinance on record" error). Added officials, meeting schedules, and primary-source links to each.

Mount Kisco: corrected the ban mechanism — it was adopted as filed Local Law 1-2025 (redefines "public utility" to exclude BESS and prohibits BESS in every district), not an informal zoning interpretation. Moratorium lapsed on adoption; existing systems grandfathered. Source: NYS filing receipt provided by the workgroup (Don Emerson, 7/2/26) and press coverage of the July 14, 2025 public hearing. Fixed a scope-line date typo (was "≈Aug 2026").
Category label "No BESS code" renamed to "Silent Code" per team request.
Village of Pelham: added a watch-flag noting a draft BESS local law referenced by the workgroup (6/29/26 meeting) but not yet publicly confirmed/adopted.
Confirmed Pound Ridge remains correctly excluded from the Priority list (behind-the-meter, limited transmission/grid-arbitrage value) per workgroup guidance and the 6/29/26 meeting.

Yorktown RECLASSIFIED (major): moved from “BESS Allowed” to “Restrictive BESS Code.” The workgroup GIS sheet (re-read 7/1/26) classifies Yorktown as “BESS limited to Tier 1 only,” and press coverage of the Town Board’s May 20 vote confirms LL 10-2025 (§300-81.5) prohibits new Tier 2 (>80 kWh) systems. Updated status, scope, snapshot, summary, flags, and localLaw.

Cortlandt deadline re-verified: LL 7-2025 extends the ALL-BESS moratorium through Dec 31, 2026 (confirmed via the Town’s legal notices / LL text). The workgroup sheet still notes “expires 07/31/26,” treated as a date-entry error; dashboard keeps 12/31/26.

Re-read the workgroup “ArcGIS data BESS 2026” GIS sheet (rows last updated 6/18/26); reconciled all municipalities — no other status changes and no new municipalities. NY S5506/A8378 unchanged, still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee.

Cortlandt CORRECTION (major): LL 7-2025 confirmed to extend the moratorium through Dec 31, 2026 — not July 31, 2026 as the dashboard previously showed. Source: the Town Board's public hearing notice for December 9, 2025 (fetched from the Town's legal notices page 6/30/26) states the extension runs 'through the end of 2026.' The workgroup GIS sheet notation 'until 07-2026' was likely a date-entry error; the legal notice text controls. Updated scope, governing-law snapshot, summary, flags, calendar deadline, and priority entry accordingly. Added Nov 17 and Dec 8 Town Board meetings to the calendar.

Mamaroneck Village: confirmed adopted law is LL 7-2026 (Tier 1 ≤40/80 kWh; >80 kWh prohibited) per the workgroup GIS sheet (updated 2026-06-18). Removed the 'unconfirmed law number' flag and updated scope and summary to reflect the confirmed law. Consistent with the April 27, 2026 Board of Trustees hearing on 'PLL G of 2026' that concluded the moratorium chain.

Pound Ridge: fixed summary inconsistency — previously said the moratorium 'appears to have given way,' but the workgroup GIS sheet (authoritative) shows moratorium active through 9/24/26. Summary rewritten to clearly state the workgroup source is being treated as controlling over Carina's 'LIFTED' label.

Carina Energy tracker and workgroup GIS spreadsheet (last updated 2026-06-18) checked — no new Westchester municipalities adopted BESS rules or new public hearings surfaced since 6/29/26. NY S5506/A8378 unchanged, still in committee.

New Castle: confirmed via Carina Energy's June 2026 update that the moratorium remains ACTIVE through roughly August 2026 (not lapsed 5/31/26 as the workgroup spreadsheet suggested); the Town's online Approved Legislation page still shows no 2026 entries, so the exact extension law is unconfirmed — flagged as a possible legal gap pending direct Town Clerk confirmation. Also found the proposed permanent law has shifted toward prohibiting Tier 2/3 outright (Tier 1 ≤80 kWh only), a stricter direction than previously described; Planning Board responded to the referral March 4, 2026, Town Board vote still pending.

Mamaroneck Village: found a April 27, 2026 Board of Trustees hearing on a proposed local law ('PLL G of 2026') addressing the BESS moratorium — not previously in this dataset. Resolved the prior internal inconsistency (status field said 'overstrict' while flags said 'Status kept ACTIVE'): the moratorium chain (LL 7/11/14-2025) appears to have concluded into a permanent Tier-1-only framework, consistent with Carina's June 2026 'LIFTED' label. Status unchanged (overstrict); flags/summary rewritten for consistency. Exact adopted law number still unconfirmed.

Pound Ridge: similarly resolved a stale claim that the March 24, 2026 hearing 'extended the moratorium, so it remains active' — Carina's June 2026 update lists Pound Ridge as LIFTED, consistent with the workgroup's own 'on-site use only' description of the town's current rule. Status unchanged (overstrict); summary/flags rewritten to stop asserting the moratorium is still active.
Village of Ossining: enriched with Local Law 4-2026's 8-month term (effective ~4/22/26), implying a computed (unconfirmed) expiration around late December 2026.

Harrison: enriched with the specific tiered standard now in force, Town Code §235-95 (Tier 1 ≤80 kWh and Tier 2 ≤600 kWh permitted outright; Tier 3 >600 kWh via Special Permit Use) — updated the action-plan ask accordingly (push on Tier 3 permit criteria, not on creating tiers).

Corrected a stale state.points claim that the coalition tracks '7 active moratorium towns' — the actual current count is 4 (Bedford, Cortlandt, New Castle, Village of Ossining), with Mamaroneck Village and Pound Ridge now under permanent restrictive codes rather than active moratoriums.

Added flags to Peekskill and Yonkers noting Carina's June 2026 update lists both as ACTIVE moratoriums, in tension with this dataset's 'lapsed 5/31/26' and 'silent' characterizations respectively — left unchanged pending direct confirmation.

Re-pulled the Carina Energy NY tracker (June 2026 update) and the Google Drive 'ArcGIS data BESS 2026' workgroup spreadsheet; reviewed all ~45 towns — no other discrepancies found. NY S5506/A8378 unchanged, still in committee.

Carina Energy NY tracker refreshed June 26, 2026 reclassified three Westchester towns: Mamaroneck Village and Pound Ridge from ACTIVE to LIFTED, and Mount Pleasant (BAN) to LIFTED — dropping its Westchester active count from 8 to 5.

Verified against official town sources: Pound Ridge noticed a 2nd-extension public hearing for its BESS moratorium on March 24, 2026, and Mamaroneck Village extended its moratorium via Local Law 14-2025 (Chapter 342). Both moratoriums remain ACTIVE — statuses NOT changed; Carina's 'lifted' labels treated as aggregator lag.
Mount Pleasant kept as permanent BAN; Carina's 'lifted moratorium' is consistent with the Dec 2025 prohibition law having superseded the prior moratorium.
NY S5506 (Kavanagh) / A8378 (Levenberg) ORES standalone-storage expansion — still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee as of June 2026; not passed.
No new Westchester municipality adopted a BESS rule; no new public hearing/vote surfaced for the other monitored towns (Cortlandt, New Castle, Bedford, Peekskill, Yonkers).
Re-confirmed every town's status against the Carina Energy NY tracker, which was refreshed June 14, 2026 (now 104 active NY moratoriums statewide; Westchester tied for the most with 8).

Tracker cross-check for the 8 Westchester active-moratorium towns held: Cortlandt, New Castle, Pound Ridge (ALL_BESS), Bedford, Peekskill, Mamaroneck (Village), Yonkers — all ACTIVE; Harrison and Yorktown remain EXPIRED; Port Chester remains off the moratorium list (regulated).

Noted tracker nuances: the moratorium tracker now lists Mount Pleasant as ACTIVE and Mount Kisco as EXPIRED, while this dashboard reports each as an effective BAN based on their own town actions (Mount Pleasant's Dec 2025 prohibition law; Mount Kisco's Aug 2025 'not a public utility' interpretation). Statuses kept as bans; see flags.
NY S5506 (Kavanagh) / A8378 (Levenberg) — ORES standalone-storage expansion — still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee; not passed either chamber.
No new Westchester municipality appeared on the tracker; no new public BESS hearing/vote surfaced for any monitored town since the prior run.
Baseline dashboard build prior to daily-refresh changelog tracking.

deadlines.label: Cortlandt — BESS moratorium expires 12/31/26 per LL 7-2025 (engage before year-end)

priority.name: Town of Cortlandt (Cortlandt Manor)
priority.why: Active moratorium on ALL BESS expires 12/31/26 (LL 7-2025); large town; High grid capacity (100% yellow). Next Town Board: July 21, 2026.
priority.action: Engage Town Board now — push a Croton-style tiered code so permanent rules allow Tier 2+ rather than defaulting to the current Tier 3 prohibition.
priority.engage: Town Board meets monthly on a Tuesday, 7:00 PM, Nyberg Room, Town Hall (1 Heady St). Use the public-comment period. Watch the town's Legal Notices / Public Hearings page for the BESS hearing before the 7/31/26 expiry; if none is posted, email the Town Clerk to be added to notifications.

priority.name: Town of New Castle (Chappaqua / Millwood)
priority.why: Moratorium lapsed 5/31/26; tiered law proposed but not adopted — open window now; Moderate capacity.
priority.action: Comment at Town Board to shape thresholds toward a permissive Tier 1 and reasonable Tier 3.
priority.engage: Town Board work sessions & meetings at 200 S. Greeley Ave, Chappaqua. The draft BESS law is on the town's Proposed Legislation page. Email the Town Clerk to be notified of the hearing and comment at the work session.

priority.name: Town / Village of Harrison
priority.why: Moratorium expired; code already allows to Tier 3 by permit; big commercial base; High capacity — high impact, low friction.
priority.action: Lock in / formalize the tiered standards and build relationships before any reactive rollback.
priority.engage: Town/Village Board meets 1st & 3rd Thursday, 7:00 PM (1 Heineman Pl). Public comment at meetings; agendas on the board page. For a hearing, check board agendas or email the Clerk.

priority.name: City of Peekskill
priority.why: Tier 1 only with the Tier 2 moratorium just lapsed (5/31/26) — clear opening; Hudson city; High capacity.
priority.action: Advocate a Tier 2 standard at Common Council now.
priority.engage: Common Council meets Mondays (work session then meeting) at City Hall, 840 Main St. Email the City Clerk; comment at Common Council. Hearings post on the city Agenda Center.

priority.name: Town of Bedford
priority.why: Large-scale moratorium until 8/17/26 (urgent deadline), but Low grid capacity (100% red) tempers payoff.
priority.action: Engage before 8/17/26 to add a Tier 2 pathway; weigh effort vs. limited grid value.
priority.engage: Town Board meets 1st & 3rd Tuesday at the Town House (321 Bedford Rd, Bedford Hills); public sessions at start/end, Zoom available. Agendas on the Agenda Center; watch for the large-scale BESS hearing before 8/17/26.

priority.name: Village of Rye Brook
priority.why: Restrictive — Tier 1 only (no Tier 2 pathway); High grid capacity, making it a strong target to loosen.
priority.action: Push the Board of Trustees to add a Tier 2 pathway with siting and safety standards.
priority.engage: Board of Trustees meets 2nd & 4th Tuesday at Village Hall (938 King St). Agendas/video on the CivicWeb meeting portal; reach officials via the Village Administrator's office.

notify.who: Shari Melillo, Secretary to the Administrator

priority.name: Village of Mamaroneck
priority.action: Advocate raising the cap / adding a Tier 2 pathway.
priority.engage: Board of Trustees meets 2nd & 4th Monday (work session ~5 PM, regular ~8 PM), Village Courtroom (169 Mt. Pleasant Ave). Agendas on the village site; comment at the meeting.

priority.name: Village of Ossining
priority.why: Fresh 8-month moratorium (ends ~Dec 2026) — good timing, but Low grid capacity (100% red) limits impact.
priority.action: Engage the Village Board to steer toward 'allowed,' but prioritize higher-capacity towns first.
priority.engage: Board of Trustees at 16 Croton Ave; the 8-month moratorium study (to ~Dec 2026) is the window. Comment at Board meetings; email the Clerk to be notified of the BESS hearing.

priority.name: Village of Briarcliff Manor
priority.why: Restrictive — prohibits Tier 2 (>600 kWh); Low capacity (75% red).
priority.action: Push to allow Tier 2 with siting standards when capacity allows.
priority.engage: Village Board meets at 1111 Pleasantville Rd; agendas on the village site. Comment at Board meetings; email the Clerk to be added to BESS/hearing notices and request a Tier 2 amendment.

priority.name: Village of Larchmont
priority.why: Restrictive — LL 2-2026 prohibits Tier 2 (>600 kWh); High grid capacity (100% yellow).
priority.action: Push to add a Tier 2 pathway with siting/safety standards.
priority.engage: Board of Trustees meets at 120 Larchmont Ave; agendas on the village site. Comment at Board meetings about adding a Tier 2 pathway; email the Clerk for hearing notices.

hearingNote: Specific public-hearing dates are not centrally published — towns post them as one-off legal notices (often via eCode360 or the town-clerk page). Recurring board meetings below are computed; confirm exact hearing dates per town. Gaps to research: firm 2026 hearing dates for Cortlandt, New Castle, and Ossining Village.
hostingNote: Grid hosting capacity is a proxy read from the ConEd hosting-capacity map colors in the workgroup sheet: more green/yellow = more available capacity (better BESS siting value); orange = limited; red = constrained. NYSEG-territory towns aren't on that map. Confirm against the utility legend before relying on it.
